title: silene acaulis subsp. exscapa
intro: Silene acaulis subsp. exscapa, also known as Mountain Catchfly, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against infections and promote overall well-being. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
